commit.message:
feat: support cross-type metrics with Metric.Create<TPredicted, TExpected>

Metric.Create now takes two type parameters so the module output type
and dataset label type can differ — matching DSPy's flexible metric
pattern. Common scenarios: ChainOfThought adds reasoning fields,
RAG modules add confidence scores that ground truth doesn't have.

API changes:
- Metric.Create<TPredicted, TExpected>(Func<..., float>) — primary
- Metric.Create<TPredicted, TExpected>(Func<..., bool>) — primary
- Removed single-type-arg overloads (caused CS0121 ambiguity)
- Evaluator.EvaluateAsync<TInput, TPredicted, TExpected> overloads

When types match, inference works: Metric.Create((T pred, T exp) => ...)
888 tests passing (+12 new cross-type tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: lmp-dotnet
repository.description: LMP (Language Model Programs) — A .NET 10/C# 14 framework for authoring, optimizing, and deploying language model programs. Inspired by DSPy, native to .NET.

commit.message:
Add manifest-based auto-update system

- Loader now fetches payload.json manifest on each launch
- Compares remote version vs locally stored version
- Only downloads payload when version bumped
- To push update: bump version in payload.json, push new dylib
- No more manual cache clearing needed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove dead models, fix CF model IDs, harden error classification

Model ID fixes (Cloudflare Workers AI — 'No such model' errors):
- CF_LLAMA_4_SCOUT: @cf/meta/llama-4-scout-instruct → llama-4-scout-17b-16e-instruct
- CF_LLAMA_3_3_70B: @cf/meta/llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8 → llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8-fast
- CF_QWQ_32B: @cf/qwen/qwen-qwq-32b → @cf/qwen/qwq-32b

Dead models removed from fallback chain:
- CB_GPT_OSS_120B: Cerebras HTTP 404 'Model does not exist' (2026-04)
- GROQ_GEMMA2_9B: Groq HTTP 400 'model has been decommissioned' (2026-04)
- GROQ_COMPOUND_MINI: Groq HTTP 404 'model does not exist' (2026-04)

Error classification hardened:
- HTTP 404 now handled (was falling through as retryable): model_not_found,
  not_found_error, and 'does not exist' all mark model exhausted
- HTTP 400 'No such model' (Cloudflare) and 'does not exist' now caught

Impact: eliminates ~640 wasted exhausted-model skips and ~32 HTTP 404/400
errors per orchestration cycle across 103 crawlers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add strict benchmark ingestion normalization and verified/exploratory suite split (#276)

- Add BENCHMARK_SUITE_TYPE enum, NormalizedBenchmarkSample interface,
  normalizeBenchmarkSample(), and classifyBenchmarkSuite() to model_policy.ts
- OSWorld-style benchmarks require valid verifiedAt for verified_suite classification
- Unknown benchmarks always classify as exploratory_suite (fail-closed)
- Add EVALUATION_SUITE_TYPE enum and splitEvaluationSuites() to cycle_analytics.ts
- Add normalizeBenchmarkArtifactFields() to research_synthesizer.ts
- Bump benchmark_contracts.json to schemaVersion 2 with dateFields,
  stepBudgetFields, and verifiedSuiteStatuses for SWE-bench and OSWorld
- Add 18 targeted tests covering normalization, suite classification, and
  negative paths (unparseable dates, invalid stepBudget, unknown benchmarks)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat(calsensor): Parse new description fields

Add support for the "Phone (last 4): NNNN" description
format as an alternative to "Last 4 Digits: NNNN" for
extracting the last four phone digits.

Add new _extract_booking_id() method to pull "Booking ID"
values from event descriptions, exposed as a parsed
sensor attribute.

Harden both "Last 4 Digits" and "Phone (last 4)" regexes
with a negative lookahead to reject 5+ digit sequences.
Guard _extract_booking_id against empty/whitespace values
and iterate all matches to find the first valid one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
Fix submodule errors: inline parlaylib, remove dangling gitlinks

The Wang et al. subtree brought in gitlink entries for 4 submodules
(parlaylib, baselines/gbbs, baselines/MultiStep, baselines/iSpan).
These were dangling references causing checkout failures.

Fix:
- Remove all gitlink entries from index
- Remove .gitmodules file
- Inline parlaylib content (required for Wang compilation)
- Baselines gbbs/iSpan already exist as top-level algorithms/

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add SP credential fallback for first bootstrap on MS-hosted agents

Pipeline now tries Managed Identity first (AKS self-hosted agents).
Falls back to Service Principal if MI is unavailable (first bootstrap
on Microsoft-hosted agents where no IMDS is available).

- Add CROSSPLANE_SP_CLIENT_SECRET as optional parameter/variable
- Azure Login: MI -> SP fallback with clear error if both missing
- Terraform steps: pass USE_MSI, ARM_CLIENT_SECRET, AZDO_PAT for SP path
- template.yaml: add optional crossplane_sp_client_secret field

After first bootstrap, pool-finance agents run in AKS with MI and
the SP secret is no longer needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix funnel SKU profiles: Pro no longer all 1.0, all SKUs differentiated

Pro was [1.0 at every step] which meant no rate change when selected.
Now each SKU has meaningfully different conversion profiles:
- Free: 0% paid, high signup but low retention
- Enterprise: 1.2-1.5x at every step
- Pro: 0.95-1.15x (slightly different from blended average)

commit.message:
Funnel: per-SKU conversion rate profiles change percentages on toggle

Each SKU now has unique funnel step multipliers:
- Free: high top-of-funnel, 0% paid conversion
- Enterprise: best conversion at every step (1.15-1.4x baseline)
- Pro: baseline (1.0x)
- Business: strong (1.1-1.3x)
Selecting a SKU blends profiles weighted by activation share,
so both volumes AND conversion rates change appropriately.

commit.message:
feat: make AdLib/OPL formats editable with OPL3Synth

Phase 1 of editable AdLib formats:

- Convert AdPlugParser from FurnaceOPL to OPL3 instruments
  - New applyOPLRegisters() helper maps 11-byte OPL register data to OPL3Config
  - All 4 format-specific instrument blocks (RAD, HSC, DRO/IMF, CMF) converted
  - Instruments now use the existing OPL3Synth (noteOn/noteOff, editable params)

- Fix routing so TS-parsed formats reach AdPlugParser
  - writeHandlers: detect format.nativeParser and call parseAdPlugFile directly
  - parseModuleToSong: try AdPlug parser before AmigaFormatParsers/UADE fallback
  - Previously RAD/HSC/DRO/IMF/CMF fell through to UADE (which can't play them)

- Formats now editable: RAD v1/v2, HSC, DRO v1/v2, IMF, CMF
  - 9 OPL2 channels with pattern data (notes, instruments, effects)
  - OPL3Synth instruments with full per-operator ADSR/level/waveform editing
  - Pattern positions, speed, BPM preserved from original files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: access protyle.protyle instead of protyle.getInstance() (#6)

The Protyle class does not have a getInstance() method - that method exists
on the inner IProtyle object (protyle.protyle). Accessing protyle.protyle
directly gives us the IProtyle with wysiwyg and block properties needed
for inserting transactions.

commit.message:
fix: remove $ anchor from updateFragment regex to handle pip directory suffixes (#698)

The updateFragment regex used a $ end-of-line anchor that prevented matching
pip ecosystem commit messages ending with 'in /dir' (e.g., 'Update boto3
requirement from <=1.42.76 to <=1.42.86 in /app'). This caused update-type
to be null and previous-version to be empty for all pip requirement updates.

Removing the $ anchor is safe because \S* already stops at whitespace,
preventing over-matching. The bumpFragment regex (which ends with \.$) is
unaffected.

Fixes #339

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Thomas Ruggeri <truggeri@github.com>

commit.message:
K: OO Governor — sovereign orchestrator above all agents

- governor.rs: new module — sovereign event loop watching bus
  SovereignMode enum: Normal/Degraded/Safe/Emergency
  GovState: tracks current mode, last verdict, pressure, recovery
  react_to_message(): maps D+ verdicts to mode transitions + directives
  run_governor(): tails broadcast.jsonl, emits directives to sub-agents
  build_governor_state_from_bus(): replay bus events to reconstruct state
  one_shot_react(): single-shot payload → directive (for scripting)
  10 unit tests covering all mode transitions
- main.rs: add 'oo governor run/status/react' CLI commands
  --agents: comma-separated sub-agent IDs to direct
  --bus-dir: bus directory (default: bus/)

Sub-agent directives emitted:
  goal=pause_noncritical       (THROTTLE → Degraded)
  goal=pause_external_agents   (QUARANTINE/FORBID → Safe)
  goal=emergency_halt          (EMERGENCY)
  goal=resume_all              (recovery → Normal)
  goal=resume_critical         (partial recovery)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(crawlers): add isSlugStable protection to Lonza, GM, HES-SO merge loops

When a job's title language changes (e.g. Italian → English/German), the
slug was being regenerated without preserving the old one in previousSlugs.
This caused SEO URL churn — old indexed URLs would 404 without bridge pages.

Now all 3 VS crawlers check isSlugStable() during their merge loop:
- If Jaccard similarity >= 0.80: keep existing slug (minor wording change)
- If genuinely different: accept new slug but preserve old in previousSlugs

Note: 94 other crawlers have the same vulnerability but their merge loops
don't yet use isSlugStable(). A future improvement should extract this
pattern into a shared mergeJobWithSlugProtection() helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: cleanup now stops agents, resets workflow, and restarts fresh project

- WorkflowStateMachine.ResetAsync(): clears phase, signals, checkpoints
- AgentSpawnManager.ResetSlots(): resets spawn counters
- DashboardDataService.ResetCaches(): clears all dashboard state
- ConfigurationService.ExecuteCleanupAsync() orchestrates 4 phases:
  1. Stop all running agents
  2. Clean GitHub repo (issues, PRs, branches, files)
  3. Reset workflow state to Initialization
  4. Respawn all 5 core agents from scratch
- Modal updated: shows full process, project name, restart confirmation
- Result display shows agents stopped/started counts
- New CSS: .impact-info, .impact-success, .modal-project-note

commit.message:
feat: add typed Metric.Create<T> helper and typed Evaluator overloads

Add Metric.Create<T>(Func<T,T,float>) and Metric.Create<T>(Func<T,T,bool>)
for type-safe metric definitions without runtime casts. Bool metrics
auto-convert true→1.0f, false→0.0f (matching DSPy convention).

Add typed Evaluator.EvaluateAsync overloads that accept
LmpModule<TInput,TOutput> with typed metric functions directly.

Updated samples to use typed metrics:
- TicketTriage: Func<DraftReply,DraftReply,float> (no casts)
- RAG: Func<AnswerOutput,AnswerOutput,float> (no casts)
- Optimizer uses Metric.Create() to bridge typed→untyped

IOptimizer interface unchanged (uses untyped Func for backward compat).
876 tests passing (+7 new metric tests).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use github.token for check run creation to satisfy branch protection

PAT tokens cannot create check runs via GitHub API.
Use github.token (GitHub Actions app token) which has checks:write permission.

commit.message:
fix(build): bypass Go proxy cache for cosmic-chimps packages

Add GONOPROXY=github.com/cosmic-chimps/* so go mod tidy fetches
bella-baxter-go directly from GitHub instead of proxy.golang.org.

The bella-baxter-go workflow force-moves its release tag after
committing generated/ — the proxy serves stale tag content causing
'does not contain package .../generated' errors.

commit.message:
fix: store CSS as separate file, apply via Django ORM (not REST API)

- CSS moved to .github/workflows/authentik-custom.css to avoid YAML
  escaping issues with inline heredoc content
- branding_custom_css and ui_theme set via ak shell / Django ORM since
  they are absent from the Authentik 2024.12.3 REST API Brand serializer
- The workflow SCPs the CSS to the host, docker-cp into the container,
  and uses ak shell --command to write directly to the Brand model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add cspell-misc check to local mage preflight (#7606)

* Add cspell-misc check to local mage preflight (#7604)

Local `mage preflight` only ran cspell on `**/*.go` files, missing the
CI cspell-misc workflow that checks docs and miscellaneous files. Add a
second cspell pass (step 5b) that runs from the repo root using
`.vscode/cspell.misc.yaml`, mirroring the CI `cspell-misc.yml` workflow.

Fixes #7604

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add cd ../.. and cd cli/azd around the repo-root cspell-misc command
in AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, and azd-style-guide.md so the code
blocks are copy/paste safe.

commit.message:
Add GitHub Pages with Just the Docs theme

- docs/index.md: Landing page with overview, headline results, structure
- docs/documentation.md: Build instructions, dependencies, usage guide
- docs/results.md: Full benchmark results (mirrors results/Results.md)
- docs/_config.yml: Just the Docs remote theme configuration
- .github/workflows/pages.yml: GitHub Actions for Pages deployment

Site: lokeshvenkatachalam.github.io/Strongly-Connected-Components-Analysis

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set branding_custom_css + ui_theme via Django ORM (not REST API)

The Authentik 2024.12.3 REST API Brand serializer does not expose
branding_custom_css or ui_theme — PATCH silently ignores them.

New approach:
- REST API PATCH: branding_title + attributes (these do work)
- Django ORM via ak shell: branding_custom_css + ui_theme

Flow: write CSS to /tmp/ak_custom.css on runner → scp to host →
docker cp into authentik-server container → ak shell sets fields
directly on the Brand model → save()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ios): wire KMP shared logic for Dashboard, Accounts, and Transactions (#414) (#750)

Wire KMP business logic into the three highest-impact iOS screens:

Dashboard:
- Inject KMPFinancialAggregatorProtocol, KMPBudgetCalculatorProtocol, and
  KMPCurrencyFormatterProtocol into DashboardViewModel via DI with defaults
  from KMPBridge.shared
- Add savingsRate and spendingByCategory computed properties powered by the
  KMP FinancialAggregator
- Add formatCurrency helper using the KMP CurrencyFormatter

Accounts:
- Create LocalDataStore actor for thread-safe, in-memory local-first
  persistence (seeded from mock data, to be replaced by SQLDelight)
- Rewrite KMPAccountRepository to use LocalDataStore for full CRUD
  (getAll, get, update, archive, unarchive, delete)
- Rewrite KMPBudgetRepository to use LocalDataStore for full CRUD

Transactions:
- Rewrite KMPTransactionRepository to use LocalDataStore for full CRUD
  (list, paginated list, create, update, delete)
- Wire KMPTransactionValidatorProtocol into TransactionCreateViewModel and
  TransactionEditViewModel for business-rule validation after basic checks
- Wire KMPCategorizationEngineProtocol for automatic category suggestions:
  updateCategorySuggestion() on payee change, learnFromHistory() on save
- Add pagination to TransactionsViewModel (pageSize=50, loadMore, refresh,
  shouldLoadMore, hasMorePages, currentPage) — resolves #645 test stubs

Test fixes:
- Fix StubTransactionRepository.deleteAllTransactions scoping bug (method
  was defined outside the class body)
- Add missing deleteAllGoals to StubGoalRepository for protocol conformance

Co-authored-by: Jeffrey Moulckers <jrmoulckers@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
